name: Polars
summary: Blazing-fast DataFrame library written in Rust with a lazy execution engine, multithreaded and columnar by default for analytical workloads.

sections.name: I/O

functions.purpose: Read a CSV file into an eager DataFrame
Read a Parquet file into a DataFrame with full type inference
Write a DataFrame to a CSV file
Write a DataFrame to a Parquet file with compression

gotchas: pl.read_csv() infers types from the first 100 rows by default; columns with mixed types later in the file may fail with a SchemaError. Use infer_schema_length=None to scan the full file.
Polars uses eager I/O by default; for files larger than RAM, use pl.scan_csv() or pl.scan_parquet() to build a lazy query plan and avoid loading everything into memory.

sections.name: Selection and Filtering

functions.purpose: Select columns by name or expression from a DataFrame
Filter rows based on a boolean expression
Add or modify columns without mutating the original DataFrame
Remove one or more columns from a DataFrame

gotchas: Polars DataFrames are immutable; methods like with_columns() return a new DataFrame rather than modifying in place. Reassign the result to a variable.
Column expressions in filter() are lazy and must use pl.col() references; passing a plain Python boolean will not filter rows and may raise a TypeError.

sections.name: GroupBy and Aggregation

functions.purpose: Group rows by one or more columns for aggregation
Sum aggregation expression for a column
Mean aggregation expression for a column
Count aggregation expression for a column

gotchas: Polars does not preserve row order after group_by() by default; use maintain_order=True if downstream logic depends on the original group sequence.
Aggregating on nullable columns without handling nulls explicitly can produce unexpected results; use fill_null() inside the aggregation expression for deterministic behavior.

sections.name: Lazy API

functions.purpose: Create a lazy DataFrame from a CSV file without loading data into memory
Convert an eager DataFrame to a lazy DataFrame for query optimization
Execute the lazy query plan and materialize the result as an eager DataFrame
Execute the lazy query plan on a small subset of data for fast inspection

gotchas: Lazy queries are only optimized when collect() is called; intermediate transformations on a LazyFrame do not trigger computation and cannot be inspected for correctness until materialized.
pl.scan_csv() on a remote URL will download the entire file during schema inference unless schema is provided explicitly; large remote files should be cached locally before scanning.
